---
title: Intake forms
description: Let non-members submit structured requests that become issues in your workflows.
---

Intake forms are the front door to your workflows. Someone who doesn't have a Kiket seat — a customer, a vendor, an internal requester — fills out a form; it becomes a new issue in a workflow's initial state. No seat required on the submitter side.

## File location

`.kiket/intakes/*.yaml`. One file per intake form. Each form is addressable at a stable URL: `https://app.kiket.dev/intake/<project-key>/<form-key>`.

## Minimum form

```yaml
key: contract-request
name: Request a contract review
workflow: contract_review
initial_state: intake

fields:
  - key: counterparty
    label: Counterparty
    type: text
    required: true
  - key: total_value_usd
    label: Contract value (USD)
    type: number
    required: true
  - key: contract_type
    label: Contract type
    type: select
    options: [msa, sow, nda, other]
    required: true
  - key: summary
    label: Brief summary
    type: textarea
```

When submitted, Kiket creates an issue in the `contract_review` workflow with these fields pre-populated.

## Field types

| Type | Renders as |
|------|-----------|
| `text` | Single-line input |
| `textarea` | Multi-line input |
| `number` | Numeric input |
| `date` | Date picker |
| `select` | Single-select dropdown |
| `multiselect` | Multi-select with chips |
| `checkbox` | Single checkbox |
| `file` | File upload (stored as an attachment) |
| `email` | Email with validation |
| `url` | URL with validation |

## Validation

Per-field:

```yaml
- key: total_value_usd
  label: Contract value (USD)
  type: number
  min: 0
  max: 10_000_000
  required: true
  help: Approximate USD equivalent.
```

## Conditional fields

Show a field only when another matches a predicate:

```yaml
- key: contract_type
  type: select
  options: [msa, sow, nda, other]
- key: other_description
  type: text
  visible_when: "contract_type == 'other'"
  required_when: "contract_type == 'other'"
```

## Branding

Each form can carry a logo, accent color, and intro markdown:

```yaml
branding:
  logo: https://example.com/logo.png
  accent: "#4f46e5"
  intro: |
    Thanks for engaging with Acme Legal.
    Fill this out and we'll respond within 24 hours.
```

## Submission confirmation

After submit, show:

- A simple thank-you screen (default), or
- The issue's public URL (if you've enabled public-issue view), or
- A redirect to an external URL.

```yaml
confirmation:
  kind: redirect
  url: https://example.com/thanks
```

## Security

- Forms run on a **separate public subdomain** so they don't share cookies with the main app.
- CAPTCHA is available (Cloudflare Turnstile); toggle via `captcha: true`.
- Per-field rate limits are enforced server-side.
- Attachments go to isolated object storage; they're scanned before anyone in the workflow can open them.
